title: sambucus nigra var. pendula
intro: Sambucus nigra var. pendula, also known as the Black Elder or Weeping Elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Sambucus nigra var. pendula include its ability to reduce congestion, soothe sore throats, and provide relief from menstrual cramps and other menstrual-related symptoms. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as elderberry syrup, tinctures, and teas, which are often used to prevent and treat respiratory infections, as well as to promote overall well-being.
